steps.name: Firebase config and initialization module
steps.criteria: Given the app starts, when the Firebase config module is imported, then it exports a valid Firestore instance configured with offline persistence and a valid Auth instance; Given Firebase has already been initialized, when the config module is imported again, then it reuses the existing app instance rather than creating a duplicate
steps.scenario_name: firebase config exports valid firestore and auth instances

steps.name: Email link auth service
steps.criteria: Given a user provides a valid email, when sendSignInLink is called, then a sign-in link is sent to that email address; Given a user clicks a valid sign-in link, when handleSignInLink is called with the URL, then the user is authenticated and getCurrentUser returns the authenticated user; Given the auth state changes, when onAuthStateChanged has a registered callback, then the callback is invoked with the current user or null
steps.scenario_name: email link auth sends link and handles callback

steps.name: Firestore staple storage adapter
steps.criteria: Given a Firestore staple storage is created with a db and uid, when save is called with a staple item, then the item is persisted to the users/{uid}/data/staples document; Given staples exist in Firestore, when loadAll is called, then all staple items are returned; Given a staple exists, when remove is called with its id, then the item is removed from storage; Given a staple exists, when update is called with modified data, then the stored item reflects the changes; Given staples exist, when search is called with a query, then only matching items are returned; Given staples exist in an area, when updateArea is called with old and new area names, then all matching items have their area updated
steps.scenario_name: firestore staple storage implements staple storage port

steps.name: Firestore area storage adapter
steps.criteria: Given a Firestore area storage is created with a db and uid, when saveAll is called with a list of areas, then the areas are persisted to the users/{uid}/data/areas document; Given areas exist in Firestore, when loadAll is called, then all area names are returned
steps.scenario_name: firestore area storage implements area storage port

steps.name: Firestore section order storage adapter
steps.criteria: Given a Firestore section order storage is created with a db and uid, when saveOrder is called with an ordered list, then the order is persisted to the users/{uid}/data/sectionOrder document; Given an order exists in Firestore, when loadOrder is called, then the stored order is returned; Given no order exists, when loadOrder is called, then null is returned; Given an order exists, when clearOrder is called, then loadOrder returns null afterward
steps.scenario_name: firestore section order storage implements section order storage port

steps.name: Data migration service from AsyncStorage to Firestore
steps.criteria: Given a user has data in AsyncStorage and no data in Firestore, when migrateToFirestore is called, then all staples, areas, and section order are copied from AsyncStorage adapters to Firestore adapters; Given Firestore already contains data for the user, when migrationNeeded is called, then it returns false and no migration occurs; Given AsyncStorage is empty, when migrateToFirestore is called, then the migration completes without error and Firestore remains empty
steps.scenario_name: migration service transfers data from async storage to firestore

steps.name: Wire Firestore adapters into app initialization
steps.criteria: Given a user is authenticated, when the app initializes, then Firestore adapters are created and injected into domain services for staples, areas, and section order while TripStorage continues to use AsyncStorage; Given a user is not authenticated, when the app initializes, then the app signals a needs_auth status and does not create Firestore adapters; Given a user is authenticated and migration is needed, when the app initializes, then data is migrated from AsyncStorage to Firestore before services become available
steps.scenario_name: initialization creates firestore adapters when authenticated

steps.name: Web app scaffold with Vite, React, Firebase
steps.criteria: Given the web directory exists, when npm run build is executed in web/, then a production build completes without errors; Given the web app is loaded in a browser, when the page renders, then Firebase is initialized and an auth hook reports the current authentication state
steps.scenario_name: web app builds and renders with firebase initialized

steps.name: Read-only staple list on web
steps.criteria: Given a user is authenticated on the web app, when the staple list page loads, then all staples from Firestore are displayed grouped by area in a desktop-optimized table; Given staples are updated in Firestore by the mobile app, when the web app is open, then the staple list updates in real time without a page refresh; Given a user is not authenticated, when the staple list page is accessed, then the user is prompted to sign in
steps.scenario_name: staple list renders items from firestore grouped by area

steps.name: useAuth hook for mobile app
steps.criteria: Given the app starts, when useAuth is called, then it returns the current auth state (user or null) and loading status by listening to AuthService.onAuthStateChanged; Given the auth state changes from null to a user, when the listener fires, then useAuth returns the updated user

steps.name: Login screen component
steps.criteria: Given the user is not authenticated, when the login screen renders, then it shows an email input and a send link button; Given the user enters a valid email and taps send link, when the button is pressed, then sendSignInLink is called with the email and a confirmation message is shown; Given the user enters an invalid email, when the button is pressed, then an error message is shown
steps.scenario_name: login screen sends sign-in link for valid email

steps.name: Wire auth into App.tsx with deep link handling
steps.criteria: Given the user is not authenticated, when App renders, then the LoginScreen is shown instead of AppShell; Given the user is authenticated, when App renders, then useAppInitialization receives the auth user and the main app loads with Firestore adapters; Given the app receives a deep link with a sign-in URL, when the link is handled, then handleSignInLink is called and the user becomes authenticated
steps.scenario_name: app shows login when not authenticated and main app when authenticated
